reason_for_decision: The article passes as a beginner guide because it explains AESA clearly, keeps the architecture claims disciplined, and avoids turning the acronym into a magic-performance label.
strengths: The article is beginner-friendly without becoming shallow or inaccurate.
It is strong on the distinction between AESA, electronically scanned arrays, and mechanical scan architectures.
The evaluation section usefully reminds readers to ask how the AESA architecture is actually being used in the system.
summary_assessment: This is a solid pass for a foundation-style article. It is readable, technically responsible, and especially good at correcting hype-driven misunderstandings about AESA radar.
